outlook: Motion designers face a stable but stagnant market with 2,761 open positions unchanged from last year and a solid median salary of $110,000. The role carries moderate AI risk at 46/100, as generative video tools like Sora, Runway, Kling, and Pika begin automating basic motion graphics work that previously required technical expertise. The skill evolution reflects this shift—demand is declining for traditional software proficiency like GUI development and office applications, while employers increasingly value human-centered abilities like active listening and critical thinking. Motion designers who can leverage AI tools for rapid prototyping while focusing on strategic creative direction and client collaboration will likely maintain their competitive edge as the field adapts to automated content generation.

skillPivots.headline: Develop AI video workflow expertise
skillPivots.body: Master Sora, Runway, Kling, and Pika to become the motion designer who can efficiently direct and refine AI-generated content rather than creating everything from scratch. Position yourself as the expert who understands how to prompt, iterate, and post-process AI video outputs to meet brand standards.

skillPivots.headline: Build strategic creative consultation skills
skillPivots.body: Leverage the rising demand for Active Listening and Critical Thinking by transitioning from pure execution to creative strategy roles. Develop skills in client consultation, creative briefing, and cross-functional collaboration to become a motion design strategist who guides projects rather than just delivers assets.

skillPivots.headline: Master brand storytelling and narrative
skillPivots.body: Combine your motion design expertise with Reading Comprehension skills to specialize in translating complex brand narratives into compelling visual stories. Focus on developing skills in brand voice interpretation, story arc development, and multi-platform content adaptation within the brand-visual cluster.
